title: Reading News
intro: Spotlights on the authors and books behind your readers' favourites. Preview edition — the weekly issue is generated from your school's most-read titles.

items.headline: Why Julia Donaldson's rhymes hook the very youngest readers
items.author: Julia Donaldson
items.summary: Donaldson's steady rhyme and repetition let pre-readers predict the next word and 'read' along from memory — a confidence-builder before decoding clicks. Pairs well with reading aloud in pairs.

items.headline: Dog Man and the case for comics with reluctant readers
items.author: Dav Pilkey
items.summary: Pilkey, who has spoken openly about his own childhood reading struggles, designs his graphic novels for momentum — short, funny, visual. Often the book that turns a 'I don't like reading' child around.

items.headline: Onjali Q. Raúf's stories that spark a classroom conversation
items.author: Onjali Q. Raúf
items.book: The Boy at the Back of the Class
items.summary: Told through a child's-eye view of friendship and refugees, Raúf's books are popular read-aloud choices for upper-primary discussion — empathy without preachiness.

items.headline: How to Train Your Dragon: when a series carries a reader
items.author: Cressida Cowell
items.summary: Twelve books of the same world and hero give a child somewhere to keep going. Once a reader is hooked on Hiccup, the next book is the easiest recommendation you'll make.
